Such capability could developed for binary management applications for NPM and other technologies that are using similar metadata projection. """

summary: "Support for WebDAV clients (e.g. cadaver) to manage Raw repositories content"
description: """We are in the process of migrating """"raw"""" artifacts repositories currently served by Apache to NXRM3 OSS, in our existing setup we use a WebDAV client [cadaver|http://www.webdav.org/cadaver/] to allow users to upload/download/delete/etc.. their artifacts in these repositories.         It seems that accessing NXRM3 Raw repositories via webdav is not supported:         In the request log:         Since Raw repositories don't have a specific structure or index, using a more interactive tool and protocol (webdav) allows a more convinient way to manage the content.    A working alternative is to use a combination of curl commands to achieve similar results, but this seems much less user-friendly.    Also, the upload capabilities that were part of NXRM2 UI are no longer available in NXRM3, so that too is no longer an option."""

summary: "include JDK serviceability and security tools in repository manager distributions which include JRE"
description: """For OSX, Windows and Docker image distributions, Sonatype includes a JRE to run repository manager. Prior to version 3.16.0, this was an official JRE build from Oracle.    The NXRM distribution included JRE bin directory does not include typical Java Development Kit tools like jstack and keytool. ( https://openjdk.java.net/tools/index.html ) Tools like these are extremely helpful from a support and admin perspective, especially on windows where native tools that perform similar functions are not readily available.    In particular is is almost impossible to get a useful thread dump from a hung Java application on Windows without a tool like jstack.    As of 3.16.0 ( NEXUS-17876 ) the bundled JRE vendor is Azul Systems and the build is OpenJDK. Azul systems OpenJDK builds include the tools mention above, and we should be able to start including them in repository manager distributions.    h4. Expected    Bundle ALL java tools found in Azul OpenJDK builds in repository manager distributions which already include a JRE.    A user should be able to run tools like jstack and keytool using the bundled java distribution.      """

summary: "Make Auto Format Search Togglable and Add UI Search Warning"
description: """When a user goes to a 'Search --> <Format>' page in the UI e.g. /#browse/search/maven, an automatic search is performed to return 300 results for artifacts of the given format.    In cases where there are a large number of repos and/or large number of artifacts of the given format, this page can take a long time to load (>60s) and can result in a UI timeout as well as an increase in CPU usage.    The UI timeout can be resolved by increasing the request timeout, but the potential exists for the instance to experience CPU related performance problems if multiple users perform the same query at the same time.    Based on this, the option should exist for large(er) instances to disable/prevent the automatic search that is performed by visiting a format search page.    In addition to this, an explicit user search also has the potential to be expensive and can result in a timeout and/or high CPU usage, therefore the UI Search pages should carry a warning/advisory informing users of this potential and advise that they attempt to perform  specific searches using the available filters.    h4. Expected    Don't perform automatic behaviors that could have expensive performance implications.    if the solution is to not perform a search automatically when choosing a format, then make it clear in the UI that the user needs to explicitly enter some criteria and explicitly click something to perform a search instead. ( ie. don't just leave a blank screen where the user may believe some background search is happening but has not returned results yet )"""

summary: "Auto add repository realms that might be used"
description: """In NXRM3, when we add an LDAP configuration, the LDAP realm is automatically added.  This behavior does not persist for NuGet (and the NuGet API-Key Realm), Docker (and the Docker Bearer Token Realm) or npm (and the npm Bearer Token Realm).    We should make it such that when repositories of these formats are added, then we automatically add these realms to save setup a step.    I know of no downside to this behavior as long as the added realms do not by default prioritize above login realms (like local, ldap and crowd)."""

summary: "Improve handling of expired LDAP cache entries under high load"
description: """When the cached LDAP information expires Nexus Repo will make a query to the LDAP server to retrieve that user's LDAP groups.  If there are large numbers of requests coming into Nexus Repo for that user at the time this happens it will result in multiple, concurrent requests to the LDAP server to retreive the user's LDAP groups, all of which are making the exact same query.    This can overwhelm some LDAP servers, leading to authentication failures. In some cases all the threads available to the NXRM process will be consumed, resulting in NXRM instability and un-responsiveness.    We should consider adding a queuing mechanism to the LDAP code.  Identical requests that trigger cache repopulation should queue up and wait if there is already another request running to retreive this information."""

summary: "Provide a way to disable use of mirrors in p2 repositories"
description: """Back when the p2 plugin was designed, only metadata was hosted at eclipse.org update sites, artifact downloads all had to be done via mirrors.    So the Nexus p2 plugin was designed to always use mirrors if they are available, it will not try to use the main download site.      However, that no longer seems to be the case, artifacts are now present on eclipse.org download sites, as can be seen by trying this URL:    [http://download.eclipse.org/releases/2018-09/201809191002/plugins/org.eclipse.core.runtime_3.15.0.v20180817-1401.jar.pack.gz]    Which works.    So forcing use of mirrors now seems to be an artificial limitation imposed by Nexus.  The mirrors list can contain hundreds of entries, and it can change frequently.  This causes problems in corporate environments that use a host name whitelist in their firewalls.  There is no way to keep such a whitelist functioning.    Nexus should provide a mechanism to disable use of mirrors in p2 repositories.  Ideally this would be something which could be turned on and off on a per repository basis.          """

summary: "Add REST API to find old user tokens by realm"
description: """Customers want to expire old user tokens by the realm they are associated with. See NEXUS-16690    NXRM 3.17.0 added a <USER>API to delete user tokens (NEXUS-19170), but an admin still has no REST way to find the tokens to delete by age.    There should be a REST endpoint to find user tokens by realm and older than a certain age. return the userid, realm, and created date.    It would be expected the user then uses the response to determine when to call the DELETE API for NEXUS-16690.  """
comments: "I don't understand the use case here.  A user hasn't used their token in x days and you want to remove it?  Is that some security policy?  I can't imagine it's a space issue, if so, I think likely the instance has other problems.    Or is the ask just to find out who hasn't logged in (via token) in a length of time?"

summary: "NXRM separation of read and proxy priveleges"
description: """Adding the nx-repository-view-*-*-read privilege grants users permission to both read assets already in the proxy and to proxy new assets that don't currently exist in the proxy.    We would find it greatly useful if these two permissions were separated, to allow us to control the importing of assets into the environment securely.    The use case is based around system accounts from gitlab runners, jenkins runners, etc. We do not want these accounts to be able to pull new assets in ot the environment."""
comments: "This is indeed something that would be very useful to us as well. FWIW, JFrog Artifactory already supports something similar."

summary: "Collecting new metrics for NXRM Professional"
description: """Some new metrics have been added to NXRM Professional for tracking different aspects of the system that may be useful:    - *nexus.analytics.repository_type_counts*  an array of repository counts, grouped by format (maven/npm/nuget/etc) and repository type (hosted/proxy/group)  - *nexus.analytics.repository_asset_count_by_format_type*  an array of asset counts, grouped by format (maven/npm/nuget/etc) and repository type (hosted/proxy/group)  - *nexus.analytics.repository_component_count_by_format_type*  an array of component counts, grouped by format (maven/npm/nuget/etc) and repository type (hosted/proxy/group)  - *nexus.analytics.blobstore_metrics_by_type*  an array of blobstore metrics, group by blobstore type (file,s3,group)  - *nexus.analytics.format_request_rates*  an array of request rates, group by format (maven/npm/nuget/etc) and repository type (hosted/proxy/group) and response family (2xx,4xx,etc)  - *nexus.analytics.cleanup_policy_criteria*  an array of cleanup criteria counts, grouped by type  - *nexus.analytics.tasks*  an array of task counts grouped by task type  - *nexus.analytics.ui_search_count*  count of UI search requests  - *nexus.analytics.unique_user_authentications_count*  different counts of unique user authentication  - *nexus.analytics.blobstore_group_counts*  count of blobstore groups, as well as an array of counts of member blobstores  - *nexus.analytics.blobstore_type_counts*  count of each type of blobstore (File,S3,Group)  - *nexus.analytics.uptime_ms*  how long the instance has been active  - *nexus.analytics.cleanup_policy_count*  number of cleanup policies configured  - *nexus.analytics.edition*  the nxrm edition (OSS/PRO)  - *nexus.analytics.version*  the nxrm version  - *nexus.analytics.aws_environment*  if the instance is running in aws  - *nexus.analytics.is_clustered*  if the instance is running HA mode  - *nexus.analytics.nodeId*  nodeId of the nxrm instance  - *nexus.analytics.collection_period_days*  number of days data has been accumulating since last reset  """

description: """When fixing [https://issues.sonatype.org/browse/NEXUS-24098|https://issues.sonatype.org/browse/NEXUS-24098] we found the safest way to fix this was to add knowledge to the maven code of specific extensions we want to support. We found adding support for all possible multi-part extensions would require carefully handling maven classifiers.     This ticket is to replace that with a more generic solution that supports all multi-part extension types."""

There appears to be demand for a guide which walks through converting a Nexus 2 plugin to a Nexus 3 bundle.    """

summary: "Create IT OSGI/Karaf runtime dependencies aggregator pom"
description: """A number of IT runtime dependencies required by “pax-exam-*” are “assumed” to be installed in your local .m2 repo already. This assumption is valid if you have already installed the entire NXRM tree, but not valid otherwise (e.g. starting from an empty .m2/repository state). One workaround is using an aggregator pom that explicitly depends on the “assumed” IT runtime dependencies to force maven to fetch the deps locally. (see example Proof of Concept: [https://github.com/sonatype-nexus-community/nexus-repository-conda/blob/conda-it-md-dr/nexus-repository-it-deps/pom.xml#L32])    Unfortunately, this workaround <USER>codes dependency versions (and possibly transitive dependencies). There may be a way to have the NXRM build generate an equivalent aggregator pom. A generated pom would solve the stale versions problem, and be guaranteed to stay in synch with NXRM. I think this code is what builds the dependency list: [https://github.com/sonatype/nexus-public/blob/master/components/nexus-pax-exam/src/main/java/org/sonatype/nexus/pax/exam/NexusPaxExamSupport.java#L429]    Open to other ideas!    For now, I will create a new module under 'build support' (nexus-buildsupport) that will provide an aggregator pom, which can be used to ensure the “assumed” runtime IT dependencies are available in your local maven repository.    I do not expect any internal modules will need to use this pom (though it may be helpful for some internal use cases). The main customer of this pom will be external format plugin ITs.    UPDATE: I thought just adding the new """"org.sonatype.nexus.buildsupport:nexus-buildsupport-it-deps"""" pom as a dependency of the IT module would be sufficient to ensure all the runtime IT dependencies are dowloaded. Not so. However, using the """"maven-dependency-plugin->get"""" goal on that pom works fine. For an example, see: [IT Pom|https://github.com/sonatype-nexus-community/nexus-repository-conda/blob/conda-it-md-dr/nexus-repository-conda-it/pom.xml#L92]"""
comments: "Still open to other solutions if any one can suggest one. :)"

summary: "Pattern Expression for repository target shows inaccessible items"
description: """It seems like a negative (arguably a security issue) to list items in a directory that the user does not have access to.  For example, if I use the """"All but sources"""" repository target for user exampleUser, and then I open a browser and go to the repository path, I would expect to see:      - Item_I_Can_See.pom    Not        - Item_I_Can_See.pom      - Item_I_Cannot_See-sources.zip    I don't want exampleUser to even know that the sources.zip is there.  I do want them to be able to browse all other items.  """
comments: "We recognized this as an issue in NXRM3 and resolved it.  We are not currently doing work on this in NXRM2."

summary: "option to generate URL-safe user tokens for URL based authentication"
description: """Certain primitive repository formats only support URL based authentication of the form:    [http://user:password@localhost:8081|http://user:password@localhost:8081/artifactory/api/pypi/pypi-virtual/simple]    For example PyPI is known to have this problem:    [https://github.com/pypa/pip/issues/4315]    YUM auth has a similar problem.    Admins may want to enforce authenticated access to repo manager using our User Tokens feature.    Currently User Tokens may be generated with non-url safe characters which prevent using the above URL based authentication mechanism.  h4. Expected    To support primitive url based auth, there should be an optional option to generate user tokens which only contain url safe characters.    h4. Possible Workaround Some Formats    Take the user token name and value generated by Nexus and URL Encode them manually first, before adding them to the URL being used in your tool.    For example, Nexus generates these tokens:    Name Code: {{/pKsRLhn}}   Pass Code: {{/HAx3rBkdsQAmsUhc2RWt73CY8zrMJPS9e1HnYeseYbh}}    As expected, this URL will not parse correctly:    [URL Encode|https://www.urlencoder.org/] each value:    Name Code: {{%2FpKsRLhn}}   Pass Code: {{%2FHAx3rBkdsQAmsUhc2RWt73CY8zrMJPS9e1HnYeseYbh}}    All of these requests authenticate properly:      While no authentication for the same URL will fail:         h4. Windows Pip     This workaround does not solve this issue for python/pip when running on Windows, see my comment below for details."""
comments: "We would definitely benefit from this feature." [SEP] "The workaround to manually URL encode the token values seems to be a suitable workaround. Let us know if some tools still can't handle properly URL encoded URLs." [SEP] "Unfortunately, when I URL encode my username token, pip still prompts me for a user for the target repo." [SEP] "[~<EMAIL>] this must be frustrating. Not only does pip not support standard credential config, it can't even handle URL encoded urls?    Can you provide us an example character that when URL encoded for pip still fails? Also what specifically is the error?" [SEP] "Re-opening.  Unfortunately, the workaround suggested in this issue does not work on *Windows* + *PIP*.     This same pip configuration works on Macintosh, but does not work on Windows:          On Windows pip sends these credentials as:        Decoded this is:          On Macintosh pip sends this for the authorization header:        Decoded this is:        So for whatever reason windows pip is not properly decoding

summary: "Ability to remove a component"
description: """In NX2 and 3, there is currently no way to remove a component (artifact) from the front end UI.  You can remove all associated assets but the folder lingers empty.    While testing NEXUS-8136, I found this potentially will cause confusion with Docker search which will list the image as still present even if all associated assets are deleted.  [~<USER> suggested and I agree this calls for a need to delete the component totally (from the UI).    Like deleting an asset, this implementation does not need to be docker specific.  I think there's value in doing it for all types.  After talking to [~<USER> ticketing for evaluation/triage."""

One of them has a specific web proxy configured (no auth required) and the other one without web Proxy config (although there is a """"transparent"""" Proxy involved)."""

We have a story ( not public ) to try and improve the experience." [SEP] "We have reopened this public issue so as to track improving the detection of remote connectivity." summary: "REST Asset Search & Download Resource"
description: """*Background*   This feature serves as a handy bridge between searching for things and downloading them. Having a download sub-resource means that clients can make simple HTTP calls for artifacts that may or may not exist, and download them without parsing any JSON.    *Acceptance*   * This sub-resource builds on NEXUS-14603 (asset search json api), but returns:   ** A 302 redirect to download the asset bytes   *** Rationale: This has the benefit of leaving a trail in build tools' logs that explains what specific artifact was suggested as the thing to download (e.g. in the case of a """"latest"""" search), rather than simply returning bytes.   *** It's fine to reuse existing format-specific content-serving endpoints, if that's expedient (as with the asset download sub-resource)   ** If there are no search results, HTTP 404   ** If the search isn't sufficiently precise to pick a winning asset (i.e. there's no sort order and there's more than one search result), then we should return HTTP 400 (client error)   ** Request logs should make it easy to tell whether users were searching for content vs. trying to download content (e.g. because downloading is a sub-endpoint or uses a 'download' param or whatever)    *Questions/Notes*   * Partial fetch/HEAD support is of course desirable, but that will be handled by the format-specific content bytes endpoint  """
comments: "Note to self/more grooming: for Maven defaults - derive primary artifact from the pom, if the pom says 'pom' and the GAV contains jar,zip,tgz,and a pom, then it is the deployers fault, and hence the search query needs to be more specific that they do not want pom."

summary: "snapshot remover task should have option to only consider releases in hosted repositories"
description: """A organization shared Nexus instance is a central publishing point for Maven2 format SNAPSHOT artifacts. CI builds would typically publish SNAPSHOT versions of their projects many times per day and not distribute these externally.    When the Remove if Released option of the snapshot remover task is selected, the task looks in all hosted and proxy repositories for a matching release GAbV of a SNAPSHOT version.    For an instance with many proxy repositories containing release repos, this can add considerable overhead.    h4. Expected    - provide a task option to choose if releases should be checked for in hosted repositories only ( not proxy repositories )  - having this option selected should be the default value when creating the task, as it is considered much more common to have your SNAPSHOT in a release hosted repo than in some proxy repo  - when Remove if Released = true AND hosted repositories only = TRUE, proxy repositories should not be checked for matching release artifacts  """
comments: "We should consider this in the context of cleanup policies, [~<USER> - also we should take into account staging. Is it really true that a single hosted repository should be the designated spot to look for corresponding released versions?" [SEP] "The scheduled tasks will be deprecated, so I take this ticket as implementation advice for NEXUS-15456."

summary: "Provide options for proxy repository outbound requests to use preemptive authentication"
description: """Preemptive auth in general is bad (this is why clients like Maven does not do them by default).    Still, there are valid user cases when it would be perfectly acceptable to have proxy repository outbound requests which are known to require authentication to use pre-emptive auth.    - the remote is a known internal endpoint that is implicitly trusted  - the remote is known to ALWAYS require auth, instead of for just some URLs  - the connection is over a https connection  - bandwidth needs to be conserved, this type of auth only sends one request with credentials instead of sending two,  - Load (network, disk, CPU) can be reduced  - outbound request logging is reduced ( 1 instead of 2)  - latency to resolve the incoming request is reduced    Implementation Suggestions  - Suggestion that the implementation only allow sending pre-emptively if the remote url is {{https}} to help avoid leaking credentials in the clear.  - suggestion that this feature could allow whitelisting remote IP addresses, to help assure that if the remote starts responding with 302 redirects to someplace not expected, then pre-emptive auth could make sure this is not a different host  - suggestion to simply not do pre-emptive auth for ANY type of 3xx http redirect  - suggestion to optionally bulk whitelist internal IP subnet by default    This story is to flesh out the value add of this type of authentication for proxy repositories and then decide how to expose the feature to Nexus Administrators in Nexus 3."""
comments: "This seems to be needed for proxy's to private npm repositories to work.  " [SEP] "We would like to know how and when this issue will be addressed. We currently have  migrations and projects being blocked because we are unable to use Sonatype to  access private npm registries." Investigation shows that this is what will be needed for npm:  NEXUS-12456    Preemptive authentication for repository authentication is still needed by some for other reasons, so this issue remains open for that." description: """For more complex use cases, allow cleanup not by the fields defined in NXRM but by regular expression (similar to content selectors)."""

Other expectations will likely need distinct tickets but the design should leave room for expansion.    -This development was done on a branch which has other work on it, so no fix version is assigned yet but we should add one once we know which.  In the event we forget, it is also (generally) recommended to read the release notes of each release (to see if anything included will help) as well as generally just upgrading.-"

summary: "Prevent information leakage"
description: """I want to prevent information about my [gov.ssp.spacelaser:spacelaser-target-subsystem|http://blog.sonatype.com/2012/02/public-service-announcement-your-build-is-leaking-and-how-to-stop-it/] artifacts from leaking to remote repositories that I use. Nexus 2 had the feature of repository routes that made it possible for me to prevent all gov.ssp artifact requests from leaving my super secret lair.    I see that the [Repository Manager 2 to 3 Feature Equivalency|https://help.sonatype.com/display/NXRM3/Repository+Manager+2+to+3+Feature+Equivalency] lists """"Manual Routing Rules"""" as having a """"Repository 3 Equivalent"""" but I cannot figure out what that is. I've played around quite a bit now with content selectors and I can see how I can make a single repository only allow requests for gov.ssp artifacts, but I like to make one big repository group that puts all of my needed repositories together for easy configuration on the client. This approach does not allow me to prevent gov.ssp requests from some of the repositories in the group and allow the gov.ssp request for others in the group.    The equivalency page also lists """"Automatic Routing"""" as """"We will consider similar optimization features like this in the future as warranted"""", but I don't know if that means it will be like Nexus 2 routing rules.     I want to prevent information leakage in Nexus 3"""

summary: "add paging for security/users resource"
description: """Installs may either have large numbers of mapped local realm (source) users (NEXUS-19209) or have their NXRM connected to one or more external realms with many users (LDAP). In some cases an NXRM instance may have more than 100 total user records, even in the default realm.    Currently the /security/users REST API is capped at returning 100 users. ( see NEXUS-21389 for more specific limitations )    h4. Expected    Implement ability to page through users returned by /security/users/.    h4. Workaround    If the goal is to list all users NXRM has access to in an external realm such as LDAP, then the recommended way to do that is query your LDAP server directly using LDAP query tools.    Using NXRM as an LDAP user directory browse tool is not a core feature of a repository manager.    """
comments: "Hi, we request you to expedite this request as we have requirement for checking user access. We have more users and we are unable to see their access using API. Also the API can have one more extra parameter to check ""status"" field, so we can have filter to see status=active.      We are managing users,via crowd (groups) for role based for each repo so we need this API to get details from crowd"

summary: "Unable to install npm package due to external reference in package.json"

This is a known design issue with the npm client and package specification by the npmjs.org authors as explained in our knowledge base article below, with a workaround given:  [https://support.sonatype.com/hc/en-us/articles/213465048-Why-does-npm-client-need-access-to-URLs-other-than-my-private-registry-].    For this example there is no workaround even when you amend the package and upload to a hosted repo, as the group Package.json merges the dependences. Not sure if there is possibility to use routing rules when merging dependences or some other solution.    Dependencies in the Package.json of engine.io-client in the hosted repo   dependencies: {   has-cors: """"1.1.0"""",   xmlhttprequest-ssl: """"1.5.1"""",   component-emitter: """"1.1.2"""",   indexof: """"0.0.1"""",   engine.io-parser: """"1.2.1"""",   debug: """"2.1.3"""",   parsejson: """"0.0.1"""",   parseqs: """"0.0.2"""",   component-inherit: """"0.0.3""""   }    Dependencies in the Package.json of engine.io-client in the proxy repo   dependencies: {   component-emitter: """"1.1.2"""",   component-inherit: """"0.0.3"""",   debug: """"1.0.4"""",   engine.io-parser: """"1.2.2"""",   has-cors: """"1.0.3"""",   indexof: """"0.0.1"""",   parsejson: """"0.0.1"""",   parseqs: """"0.0.2"""",   parseuri: """"0.0.4"""",   ws: """"0.8.0"""",   xmlhttprequest: """"https://github.com/rase-/node-XMLHttpRequest/archive/a6b6f2.tar.gz""""   }    Dependencies in the Package.json of engine.io-client in the group repo   dependencies: {   component-emitter: """"1.1.2"""",   component-inherit: """"0.0.3"""",   debug: """"2.1.3"""",   engine.io-parser: """"1.2.1"""",   has-cors: """"1.1.0"""",   indexof: """"0.0.1"""",   parsejson: """"0.0.1"""",   parseqs: """"0.0.2"""",   parseuri: """"0.0.4"""",   ws: """"0.8.0"""",   xmlhttprequest: """"https://github.com/rase-/node-XMLHttpRequest/archive/a6b6f2.tar.gz"""",   xmlhttprequest-ssl: """"1.5.1""""   }"""

summary: "Virtual Hosts for Docker Repos"
description: """Add the ability for various docker repositories to use a shared port and then virtual hosts to identify which repository should be accessed. Will allow for much cleaner URLs (namespaces) with all registries able to share the default SSL port of 443.    Although this can be done with a reverse proxy, I have always found that reverse proxies add a layer of complexity in the pipeline and negatively impact on performance. It can be difficult to configure the proxy to match the requirements for the backend service particularly where you need to be able to handle large requests (sometimes in the range of gigabytes) - I would like to be able to service all HTTP/HTTPS needs with the inbuilt Jetty web server.    One obvious consideration for this is that support for SNI must be included where secure HTTPS connections are involved."""
comments: "Was just thinking and implementation of this feature would allow redirection of `index.docker.io` via DNS/hosts file which would be extremely useful." [SEP] "We understand this is important to you and is to us as well, however, it is not on the short term roadmap.  Please continue to watch and vote for this ticket if you desire the feature."

summary: "make configuring remote jmx access simpler"
description: """We have [an article published which describes how to configure Nexus 3.x to allow remote JMX access|https://support.sonatype.com/hc/en-us/articles/218501277-Configuring-Nexus-Repository-Manager-3-To-Allow-JMX-Connections].    This improvement request is about making configuring remote JMX access to Nexus 3.x container mbeans much easier to setup than the existing instructions.    *Existing implementation involves:*   * (possibly) editing etc/karaf/jmx.acl.cfg to change role mappings to mbean access   * using non-intuitive 'karaf-' prefixed role names for accessing all remote jmx features   * editing *etc/karaf/org.apache.karaf.features.cfg* to add management feature   * editing *etc/karaf/org.apache.karaf.management.cfg* to add rmi server host and port properties   * restarting Nexus   * {color:#ff0000}creating viewer and admin roles in nexus ui - for a non-administrator user, possibly assigning both viewer and admin roles to be an jmx admin ( makes more sense to only have to apply one of these ){color}   * assign roles to users, other roles, as required    *Areas to improve:*   * reduce manual or custom editing of properties files and replace with setup over UI instead   * do not require restarting the server   * ship with default roles which provide basic levels of read only or read/write access to mbeans so user does not need to create these themselves    *Implementation ideas:*    Add a JMX Remoting Capability that allows configuring   * rmi hostname   * ports   * management feature enablement ( capability is enabled )   * mbean viewer role name   * mbean writer role name   * allows the most common JMX configurations and has sane default values   * (advanced) custom mbean RBAC access mapping    Also, ship with some default viewer and admin roles for JMX already created and not tie this to `karaf-` prefix.     """

summary: "expose cache configurations in mbeans"
description: """Nexus 3.1.0 exposed the net.sf.ehcache.SampledCache and net.sf.ehcache.SampledCacheManager mbeans.    This was extremely useful as it gives insight into how all the caches are configured, something that is difficult to know at runtime using any other method.    Nexus 3.3.1+ has removed these mbeans and we can no longer figure out how each cache is configured - which makes it difficult diagnosing cache issues. You do have stats from the javax.cache.* mbeans, but stats are no good without the context of the configuration.    *Acceptance Criteria*  - add back similar mbeans  - the mbeans should expose data or manage the cache via calls into jcache which abstracts both ehcache and hazelcast, so that the mbeans will be useful in clustered and unclustered modes  """

summary: "Avoid N+1 database queries in OSSIndex integration"
description: """The OSSIndex integration recently added in NEXUS-24288 means that the browse UI will now make an extra database query for every node on the page. This ends up being a classic """"N+1"""" DB problem - where one query leads to N additional queries.    These extra DB queries are used to get the full component details, but in practice only the component coordinates are needed to send to OSSIndex. These coordinates are set when the component is created and will not change over time. It's therefore feasible to store the component's {{PackageUrl}} (based on the """"purl-spec"""" standard) in the browse node when the component is attached to the browse node.    This would mean only one database query was needed for each browse UI page - because the returned browse nodes would already contain the package-url for sending to the OSSIndex service."""

summary: "Allow to specify the number of items per page when using the REST API"
description: """When using the Nexus API to get lists of assets and components (-or any other endpoint that supports pagination-) the result set is limited to 10 items per page.    https://github.com/sonatype/nexus-public/blob/master/components/nexus-repository/src/main/java/org/sonatype/nexus/repository/rest/internal/resources/AssetsResource.java#L107  https://github.com/sonatype/nexus-public/blob/master/components/nexus-repository/src/main/java/org/sonatype/nexus/repository/rest/internal/resources/ComponentsResource.java#L140    This means that when using the Nexus API to query large repos the interaction is rather slow since a new request has to go back and forth for every 10 items queried. If we could customize this value and increase it, I believe this would greatly improve performance.    The /search resource is limited to 50 items per page:    https://github.com/sonatype/nexus-public/blob/master/components/nexus-repository/src/main/java/org/sonatype/nexus/repository/rest/internal/resources/SearchResource.java#L104      h4. Expected    - expose a parameter+value that the caller can specify on request that can customize the number of top level items to return per page in the response  - a REST resource that accepts this parameter must also have a documented <USER>coded maximum value that the passed parameter value will not be able to exceed ( to protect the server from abuse )  - if the specified value is larger than the <USER>coded maximum value allowed for that resource, then use the hardcoded maximum value instead of erroring - document this behavior and the maximum value so if the user wonders why they don't get more per page, they can learn why"""
comments: "[~<USER> I've refined this issue, and have made it public. The <USER>coded limits for some resources are too low to be practical, but it remains a good idea to have <USER>coded realistic maximums per resource.    The ask of allowing the caller to specify max items per page ( as long as it is lower than the maximum value ) is what I've turned this Jira into.    See NEXUS-21078 for increasing the default maximums per page" [SEP] "Great thanks <USER> The Expected criteria you added are exactly what I was hoping for." [SEP] "I created a [PR|https://github.com/sonatype/nexus-public/pull/51] to address this."

summary: "Raw proxies should support passing query parameters"
description: """We need to create internal proxies for various remote repositories, but raw proxies are currently quite simple and are missing features that would make this possible.    Raw repositories should support:   * passing query parameters from the request to the remote URL   * specifying custom query parameters to pass in all requests to the remote URL    These features are supported by other artifact repository solutions.    *Details*    +As an example:+ Cypress, a popular JavaScript testing framework, uses query parameters to determine which artifact you're requesting:  ||Request URL||Redirect URL||Filename||Size||  |[https://download.cypress.io/desktop/3.8.3?platform=win32&arch=x64]|[https://cdn.cypress.io/desktop/3.8.3/win32-x64/cypress.zip]|cypress.zip|150MB|  | [https://download.cypress.io/desktop/3.8.3?platform=darwin&arch=x64]|[https://cdn.cypress.io/desktop/3.8.3/darwin-x64/cypress.zip]|cypress.zip|160MB|  |[https://download.cypress.io/desktop/3.8.3] (no query parameters are specified and it detects my O/S)|[https://cdn.cypress.io/desktop/3.8.3/darwin-x64/cypress.zip]|cypress.zip|160MB|    Unfortunately, using the proxy repository (of [https://download.cypress.io|https://download.cypress.io/desktop/3.8.3?platform=win32&arch=x64]) yields different results:  ||Request URL||Path||Filename||Size||  |[https://nexus.com/repository/raw-proxy-cypress-download/desktop/3.8.3?platform=win32&arch=x64] |desktop/3.8.3|3.8.3|159.2MB|  |[https://nexus.com/repository/raw-proxy-cypress-download/desktop/3.8.3?platform=win32&arch=x64]|desktop/3.8.3|3.8.3|159.2MB|  |[https://nexus.com/repository/raw-proxy-cypress-download/desktop/3.8.3]|desktop/3.8.3|3.8.3|159.2MB|    (I deleted the artifact and cleared the cache between requests).    Additionally, the proxy repository caches the file as 3.8.3 instead of cypress.zip. This is covered in: https://issues.sonatype.org/browse/NEXUS-22889     """

summary: "Yum support for modules.yaml"
description: """I assumed NEXUS-21311 would support non-proxy repositories, in addition to proxy repositories.    Can we support modules.yaml in standard yum, non-proxied repositories?    The use case is offline mirroring of centos 8 or rhel 8 yum repositories that support modules.yaml.    Also, the only workaround I can fathom is switching yum to a raw repository and manually uploading the repodata; which doesn't seem very feasible.     P.S. please open source the yum repository components, so the community can assist in providing solutions for these types of issues in a timely manner."""
comments: "[~<USER> are you wanting to upload a modules.yaml to a hosted repository or are you expecting that the hosted repository will somehow auto-generate modules.yaml?" [SEP] "Unknown.  In my use case, we mirror upstream repos on air-gapped networks, but since we normally only download newest rpms, we let nexus just build new metadata automatically, instead of copying the origin's repodata.  For modules.yaml, if the content isn't contained in the rpm for recreation during indexing, then we will need to upload a modules.yaml... which basically makes using Nexus extremely painful as we will have to single-thread our uploads to ensure modules.yaml consistency."

summary: "Explain how to fix users that lock themselves out of Nexus 3"
description: """Today, we allow you to remove the Nexus authorizing or Nexus authentication realm, which (if you haven’t configured any other realms) prevents anyone (including the admin) from logging into the web UI. This is bad UX.    Demo: http://take.ms/vTBEX    Ideas for improvement:    + Combine the Nexus authenticating and authorizing realms into one.  + Make the Nexus authenticating and authorizing realms mandatory.  + Create a separate realm, tied to the admin account, which cannot be deactivated"""

summary: "Implement the git-lfs locking API"
description: """I noticed while evaluating nexus sonatype that the gitlfs locking api is not supported. (the required routes return 404, e.g. /info/lfs/locks)    My team would be highly interested in the file locking API.         You can read more about it over here: [https://github.com/git-lfs/git-lfs/wiki/File-Locking]    The spec for implementing the API: [https://github.com/git-lfs/git-lfs/blob/master/docs/api/locking.md]         Thank you for consideration.     """
comments: "This would also be useful for our project"

summary: "Increase the default maximum heap and direct memory sizes"
description: """The default maximum heap size in Nexus Repo 3 is currently 1.2Gb.  The reason for this is historical. Back when we supported 32 bit Windows in Nexus Repo 2 that was the highest heap size that could reliably be allocated when memory was fragmented.    There is no good reason for the default heap to be that small these days, and It is becoming very clear that this is not an adequate heap allocation for Nexus Repo 3. We constantly see people who have problems after upgrading to a new version because they forget to adjust heap memory size from the default values.    I propose that we make the sizing profiles here 8, 16, and 32 (increasing from the current 4, 8, and 16):    [https://help.sonatype.com/display/NXRM3/System+Requirements#SystemRequirements-GeneralMemoryGuidelines]    And make the heap and direct memory defaults what is shown for the 8Gb model.          """

summary: "Manage Plugins Admin GUI"
description: """Installing plugins is too complicated.    The process should not require an engineer (software or systems) to have admin/sudo access to the server, run/execute scripts or terminal commands, and/or modifying the internal directory structure and XML files.    Instead, a simple “Manage Plugins” GUI should be added to the administration options.    This should list all of the currently available features and plugins, as well as whether or not they are enabled or disabled.    The administrator should be able to enable and disable all features (which means that features will have to be developed in a way that allows that).    On the same page, there should be some “action” buttons that also allows the administrator to install new plugins, or completely delete them and all related data.    The “delete” option should be highly destructive, so it should also provide plenty of warnings and require the admin to do some additional confirmation (like typing in the name of the plugin, or entering their password again), and advise the admin to just “disable” the plugin instead.    The “Install” option should ask the admin whether they also want to enable the plugin “now”, or if they want to manually enable it later.    If a certain plugin requires some additional configuration before it can be enabled, then Nexus should show a “configuration” screen for acquiring that data. This does mean that plugins will be required to implement an interface so that Nexus knows how to gather such required information."""
comments: "Things have improved here.  As of version 3.15.0 plugins can be built in KAR format.  Plugins built in that manner can just be dropped into the ""deploy"" directory of the $workdir:      https://issues.sonatype.org/browse/NEXUS-12808?focusedCommentId=655223&page=com.atlassian.jira.plugin.system.issuetabpanels:comment-tabpanel#comment-655223    I'm leaving this issue open, because we do plan to make plugin management UI at some point. "

summary: "Vagrant format support"
description: """Vagrant holds entire VM images.      The public repository """"Atlas"""" has the following downsides:  * Puts proprietary data in the form of a fully working, easily deployed instance of company intellectual property in the cloud.  * Requires a paid account.  * Causes a lot of traffic to the cloud service that could be avoided by having the repo be on premise or """"within tenant"""" for clouds like AWS / Azure.    It would be great if it supported all three repo types - proxied, hosted and group.    Also I believe there are two type of repository fetches - one to get """"Vagrant"""" files - usually this is against the Atlas name space - but would be cool if a private repo supported this operation.  (""""vagrant init"""")  The other is to pull the actual *.box file that contains the VM image. (""""vagrant up"""")"""
comments: "Please also note http://ideas.sonatype.com/ideas/IDEAS-I-140" [SEP] "This would also be of interest to me. There are some thing we just can't run inside Docker containers and vagrant boxes fill this gap." [SEP] "Actually in our case the top layer is VMware and we would like still use it, on the other hand I have introduced Apache Mesos and Docker (the aim is to dockerize every application we have in our company and actually we are already almost at 70% coverage). The Nexus is product of choice for Java based apps/frameworks/libs, NodeJS artefacts, docker images for applications and Java/nodeJS microservices. Our systems are running on Scada network for public infrastructure management and therefore highly secured without physical internet connection, so I also introduced using Nexus as repo mirror for RHEL/CentOS based YUM repository.     The only remaining piece in our puzzle in the moment is to have vagrant images for vSphere(DC)/VirtualBox(local workstations) in some kind of repository as well. And if all

summary: "It should be possible to block npm metadata with routing rule"
description: """Routing rules can be used to control which npm packages can be downloaded through a proxy repository.  However, they cannot be used to block download of npm metadata requests. For instance, you can block download of:    [https://localhost/repository-npm-proxy/someproject/-/someproject-1.0.0.tgz]    But you cannot block download of:    [https://localhost/repository-npm-proxy/someproject]    For better or worse, most npm builds are configured to always pull the latest versions of components. Because you can't block download of metadata via routing, you'll end up with a broken build if the latest version is in the metadata, but blocked by routing.                    """

summary: "add ability to display approximate on disk size of assets and components per repository"
description: """Nexus administrators need to decide how to effectively manage the disk space consumed by assets and components, per repository **because all scheduled tasks which reclaim disk space are per repository.** This assists the admin in deciding how frequently to run scheduled task cleanups, or when to provision additional disk to better manage infrastructure.    *Expected:*    Per repository, display on disk estimate of disk usage by assets and components. """
comments: "Voting for this ticket, since I'm looking into my nexus disk and I'm worried that nexus is consuming a lot of space." [SEP] "Voting since this feature could be useful for better space management for our repositories." [SEP] "Taking this out of 'supportant' since we're doing NEXUS-14837 instead for now."

summary: "Serve files from inside npm packages over HTTP(s)"
description: """I use Jenkins to build and publish npm packages to nexus containing:   # static sites   # build / test reports   # js libraries    We then use Jenkins to download the artifact from nexus and deploy it to a webserver for testing.         If we could view the built sites in a browser directly from Nexus, it would eliminate the need for the websevers and the extra deploy stage.         This feature would be similar to the existing raw repository type, but would just allow us to access npm package contents over HTTP as if they were raw repositories.         I'm basically looking for the popular cdn [https://unpkg.com/] but for nexus."""

summary: "Meta Data for Library Management"
description: """As a Nexus User I want to know what libraries my organization has deprecated and information about the deprecation. For example: Date Deprecation Occurred, Replacement Library, Date the replacement library must be used.    Currently [tagging|https://help.sonatype.com/repomanager3/tagging] has a direct tie between the tag and the meta data which means that unique tags would need to be setup for each library deprecated.    A possible suggestion it to implement a version of tagging in which a tag is no longer global but a tag instance which can be applied to a repository member.     Example:   * Library abc is deprecated on 1/1/2013 and should be replaced with library efg.   * Library oke is deprecated on 12/4/2009 and should be replaced with oke.   * Library abc Tag=+Deprecated+; MetaData=deprecated.date=1/1/2013, deprecated.replace=efg   * Library abc Tag=+Deprecated+; MetaData=deprecated.date=12/4/2009, deprecated.replace= oke    This would provide the ability to search the repo for all deprecated libraries and determine the disposition for each library that has been deprecated          """
comments: "Here is some of the information we would want to have for each component.     Note: The word component is used to mean any artifact library, jar, war, js, which is stored in Nexus.    Since a TAG is currently tighty coupled with the meta data we would end up with something like this.     We would really like the TAG to be generic and the Meta Data to be tied to the component.     For now it means we need to create lots of unique tags.   |*Tag*|*Purpose*|  |DeprecatedReplaceWithURL|The component has been deprecated and is to be replaced with what is specified in the referenced URL.|  |EnterprisePOMListURL|The component exist in the enterprise POM’s listed found in the referenced URL.|  |ModuleListURL|The component is a module and exists in the areas listed.|  |TopLevel|Top level (non-transitive) component.|  |VendorProductNameVersion|Name of vendor; name of product; Version|  |Documentation URL|Document URL|  |DownloadURL|Download URL|  |LicenseLocation|License URL|  |SupportURL|Support URL|  |Commercia

summary: "ability to cleanup tags by the date the tag was last associated or disassociated on components"
description: """A request is for the addition of a """"Last used"""" property to the tag (i.e., this tag was last * applied or removed from components on mm/dd/yyyy @ hh:mm:sss *)  h4. Use Case    The task for cleaning up tags uses """"Tag creation days:"""" and """"Tag modified in days"""" as the main selection process. Due to the volume of development the count of unique tags could be quite high. Due to the varied iteration and release schedules we have, the length of time that a tag could be valid for could be range from two weeks to indefinitely.    Because of those two observations I am suggesting two changes to tagging. One change is for the tag, itself. The other is for the Admin - Cleanup tags.    The first is to detect and make visible when the tag was """"Last used"""". From a tagging perspective that would allow us to base tagging clean up on something other than the creation data and last modified date.    By adding """"Last used"""" property to the tag and as a condition in the """"Admin - Cleanup Tags"""" task, we would have a way to avoid the clean up of tags that are still part of a longer iteration or a release which is a risk when cleaning up tags based on creation or modification dates."""

summary: "Allow HTTP configuration via REST"
description: """As a user of the REST API, I have need to configure HTTP settings.    This is a ticket to track use cases and interest.    *Acceptance*    Anything doable via the UI, you should be able to do via REST."""
comments: "I could have sworn this was already a request when I looked shortly after the massive REST implementation, or else I might have opened one sooner – I would like to see the same thing implemented.  We support quite a few customer groups that, for various reasons, require their own Nexus servers, and having this one thing to configure by hand for new customers is getting silly."

summary: "Settings Templates in NXRM3"
description: """Nexus 2 has a feature called Maven Settings Templates.    https://books.sonatype.com/nexus-book/reference/maven-settings.html    This feature helps some teams onboard developers with the correct user token settings, by way of integration with the maven m2-settings plugin:    https://github.com/sonatype/nexus-maven-plugins/tree/master/m2settings    Users are looking for a similar feature in Nexus 3. The primary use case is to help more quickly and consistently setup a new developer to use Nexus Repository Manager without going to the UI directly.    This issue is to track adding a similar feature to Nexus 3.    """

summary: "Update /beta/repositories and /beta/repositories/*/* endpoints to /v1"
description: """We want to update /<USER>repositories endpoints to /v1/repositories endpoints so customers can adopt and use these endpoints without worrying about the stability stigma from having a <USER>label.    *Acceptance Criteria*   # Update */<USER>repositories* to */v1/repositorySettings* and ensure both /<USER>and /v1 prefixes can access the same API logic.   # Update the */<USER>repositories/***/** REST APIs to */v1/repositories/***/** and ensure both  /<USER>and /v1 prefixes access the same API logic.   # /<USER>APIs are hidden from Swagger UI    *Notes*   * We are not changing the REST API endpoint /v1/repositories. This endpoint lists repositories and is intended to be used by CI/CD tools like Jenkins. Not changing this endpoint to ensure we don't impact customer's existing CI/CD pipeline."""

summary: "make HTTP client preferred auth schemes configurable in response to HTTP proxy server challenges"
description: """NXRM 3 uses an ordered set of HTTP client ( outbound requests ) preferred auth schemes in response to HTTP proxy Server 407 Proxy-Authenticate challenges.     When the configured HTTP proxy authentication username contains a domain part:  - NTLM, DIGEST, BASIC  When the configured HTTP proxy authentication username does not contain a domain part and is NOT Bearer token:  - DIGEST, BASIC    https://github.com/sonatype/nexus-internal/blob/91244e0fc322fdd395a7814bc5150a2f0779ed73/components/nexus-httpclient/src/main/java/org/sonatype/nexus/httpclient/config/ConfigurationCustomizer.java#L251    IQ Server recently made their preferred outbound preferred client auth schemes configurable and changed from:    ntlm,negotiate,digest,basic    to ( HTTP client default )    Negotiate,Kerberos,NTLM,CredSSP,Digest,Basic    though this primarily affects HTTP Proxy servers returning 407 status codes.    h4. Problem    Some environments with HTTP proxy servers handling outbound requests do not allow customizing the the Proxy-Authenticate headers on a 407 response. A server may reply with all the schemes it supports ( example: Negotiate, NTLM, Basic ), though it will be up to the client to iterate all the available schemes it supports to decide which one to use. Since the preferred auth scheme order is hardcoded in NXRM, the wrong preferred scheme may be attempted before a preferred scheme. In a worse case scenario, one of the chosen schemes (ie. NTLM ) may be chosen to use but ultimately fail due to some underlying protocol issue.    h4. Expected    Allow the HTTP client preferred auth schemes to be configurable for HTTP proxy authentication replies instead of hardcoded to a fix set of values.    No need to change the preferred scheme order for responses to non-http-proxy challenges.      """

summary: "Capture stdout and stderr to logfile"
description: """Nexus 3 has no default log that captures stdout/stderr. This means that the most reliable way to get a thread dump from a hung instance (kill -3) will not work.    This is a regression from Nexus 2, and has serious implications for support. Other methods of getting thread dumps such as jstack require that a JDK is available, and even when these tools are available they are not as reliable as sending a SIGQUIT.    *Acceptance*     Output written to stdout and stderror are written to the standard logs"""
comments: "the file output should be able to handle large output - like multiple thread dumps. Consider file rotation as an option based on size, time or number of archives to retain - similar to wrapper.log in Nexus 2" [SEP] "Verified these contents now go to jvm.log whose default location is with the rest of the logs in the sonatype-work directory in NX3.1.  Thanks all!"

summary: "Support pushing Docker Windows Container images and loosen manifest validation to allow for 'foreign-layers'"
description: """I followed the instructions for setting up a Hosted Docker repository in Nexus. I'm using Windows containers on my Windows 10 machine. I created an image based off microsoft/nanoserver, which I try to push to the Hosted Docker repo in Nexus, but I am met with the following error: """"blob unknown: blob unknown to registry"""".     I switch Docker on my machine to use Linux containers, create an image, upload that image to the Hosted Docker repo in Nexus, and it succeeds.     My conclusion is that the Hosted Docker repo in Nexus is unable to store Windows Container images and there is no obvious place to configure the Nexus repo to host Windows Container images.    *Solution*  Minimally NXRM should be able to validate the pushed Manifest, taking into account that the 'foreign-layers' will never be in NXRM directly."""
comments: "The Nexus server is running on Ubuntu 16.04.1 -- will this prevent Nexus from storing Windows Container images?" [SEP] "[~<USER> Sorry for the late reply.  We currently believe this will be covered by NEXUS-11865, though will leave this tikcet open until we are completely sure." [SEP] "Thank you, in the meantime I found out about Docker Registry (https://docs.docker.com/registry/) which is what we're going to use." [SEP] "May also be relavent/helpful: http://www.sonatype.org/nexus/2017/11/28/setting-up-sonatype-nexus-3-as-your-windows-docker-container-registry" [SEP] "No go on [~<USER> testing, unfortunately he got pulled away."

summary: "Allow REST API search and download through proxy repositories"
description: """Currently a REST API search or download request against a proxy repository will only work if the components are cached locally in a proxy repository.     For instance a request to download the latest snapshot jar for a GAV:    This will fail unless the jar is already downloaded into the proxy.    Retrieving the latest version of a component via REST API through a proxy is a use case we should be supporting.  Possible implementations for this include implementing a remote search capability, or, specifically for Maven, using the maven-metadata.xml files to find available versions."""
